title: Finding Kernel of Matrix Transformation
description: Algorithm to find the kernel (null space) of a linear transformation represented by matrix A
explanation: This algorithm finds all vectors x such that Ax = 0 by solving the homogeneous system. The solution space forms the kernel/null space of the transformation.

title: Finding Image (Column Space) of Matrix Transformation
description: Algorithm to find the image/range of a linear transformation by determining column space
code:
ALGORITHM FindImage(A)
INPUT: m×n matrix A representing T(x) = Ax
OUTPUT: Basis for image of T
BEGIN
 // Method: Find pivot columns of A
 R ← row_echelon_form(A)
 
 // Identify pivot columns in original matrix
 pivots ← find_pivot_columns(R)
 
 // Pivot columns of A form basis for column space
 basis ← []
 for each pivot index p in pivots do
 basis.append(column(A, p))
 END FOR
 
 // Alternative: Transpose and find null space of A^T
 // im(T) = null(A^T)⊥ (orthogonal complement)
 
 RETURN basis
END
explanation: The image is spanned by the columns of A. By row reducing and finding pivot columns, we identify which original columns form a basis for the column space.

title: Rank-Nullity Computation
description: Compute rank and nullity using Gaussian elimination
code:
ALGORITHM ComputeRankNullity(A)
INPUT: Matrix A (m×n)
OUTPUT: rank and nullity of T(x) = Ax
BEGIN
 // Row reduce to echelon form
 R ← row_echelon_form(A)
 
 // Count pivots = rank
 rank ← count_pivots(R)
 
 // Apply Rank-Nullity Theorem
 n ← cols(A)
 nullity ← n - rank
 
 // Determine properties
 IF nullity == 0 THEN
 print "T is ONE-TO-ONE (injective)"
 ELSE
 print "T is NOT one-to-one"
 END IF
 
 IF rank == m THEN
 print "T is ONTO (surjective) to R^m"
 ELSE
 print "T is NOT onto"
 END IF
 
 RETURN (rank, nullity)
END
explanation: Uses Rank-Nullity theorem to compute both quantities. Rank is found via row reduction, then nullity = n - rank. Also determines if transformation is 1-1 or onto.
